title: Soft Fried Chicken with Hot Bean Sauce

ingredients.title: For the Chicken
items: 500g chicken breast or thigh (boneless, skinless, cut into bite-sized pieces)
2 tbsp potato starch or cornstarch (for coating)

ingredients.title: For the Hot Bean Sauce
items: 2 tbsp doubanjiang (spicy fermented broad bean paste)

ingredients.title: Aromatics & Vegetables
items: 2 scallions, white and green parts separated and chopped
1 red chili, sliced diagonally (optional for extra heat)

instruction.title: Marinate the Chicken
steps: In a bowl, combine chicken with Shaoxing wine, soy sauce, cornstarch, and egg white.
Mix until fully coated and let rest for 20–30 minutes. This tenderizes the chicken.

instruction.title: Prepare the Sauce
steps: In a small bowl, mix doubanjiang, soy sauce, sugar, Shaoxing wine, chicken stock, and sesame oil. Set aside.
Taste the sauce — it should be savory, slightly spicy, and balanced.

instruction.title: Fry the Chicken
steps: Heat oil in a wok or deep fryer to 170–180°C (340–355°F).
Lightly coat chicken pieces with potato starch.
Fry in batches until golden but still soft inside (do not over-fry). Drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: Stir-Fry Aromatics
steps: In a clean wok, heat 1 tbsp oil over medium-high heat.
Add garlic, ginger, and the white part of the scallions. Stir-fry until aromatic.
Add the sliced chili if using.

instruction.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
steps: Pour the prepared hot bean sauce into the wok with aromatics.
Bring to a simmer for 1–2 minutes.
Add fried chicken and toss gently to coat. Heat through for another minute.

instruction.title: Finish and Serve
steps: Garnish with the green parts of the scallions.
Serve immediately with steamed rice or noodles.

chefTips: Soft chicken texture: Marinate with egg white and cornstarch to get silky, tender restaurant-style mouthfeel.
Control spice: Doubanjiang is very salty and spicy — adjust according to taste.
Double frying: Fry chicken twice (160°C then 180°C) for extra crispiness outside while keeping inside soft.
Sauce consistency: It should cling lightly to the chicken. If too thin, add ½ tsp cornstarch slurry.
